title: {{routing}} placeholder in the assembly pipeline (runner + projection); model-router ships the v4 orchestrator prompt preserving [orchestrator-active] + {{capabilities}}; integration-check passes

tests.cmd: npx vitest run tests/routing-assembly.test.ts (+ full suite)
tests.summary: 6 passed — assembled orchestrator instructions contain the routing section (marker exactly once) AND [orchestrator-active] AND the Verity identity, with no {{routing}}/{{capabilities}} leak; substituteRoutingPlaceholder strips cleanly when no section + is a no-op without the placeholder (default-composition safety); resolveRoutingSection falls back to seed, prefers composition-scoped routing.json (active profile honored), returns null on invalid config. Full suite 525 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed.

integrationCheck.summary: router-v4 (selects model-router as orchestrator): [PASS] Assembled system prompt — contains the orchestrator marker + Verity identity; the assembled prompt holds '<!-- garrison:routing v1 profile=balanced -->' + '## Routing policy' (once each) + [orchestrator-active], 0 placeholder leaks. default (garrison-orchestrator, no {{routing}}): [PASS] unchanged — no regression. (Network/gateway checks require a live operative, out of scope for the gated build.)

validateFitting.summary: PASS — architecture/security/prompt-injection/quality, now with the .apm/prompts/model-router.prompt.md orchestrator prompt.

build.summary: Next build compiled — the runtime dynamic-import of routing-core.mjs (pathToFileURL) is NOT bundled, no webpack breakage

e2e.summary: N/A — assembly-pipeline slice; the committed vitest + the regenerable router-v4 assembled prompt + integration-check are the re-runnable artifacts. The own-port view e2e lands in MR2.

by: N/A — no UI surface (view is MR2)

video.note: Backend slice; MR-wave consolidated walkthrough filmed at MR2.

notes: BRIEF v4 ADAPTATION (recorded): the {{routing}} section is injected through the SAME assembly pipeline as {{capabilities}}. New runner.ts exports: substituteRoutingPlaceholder (pure replace; strips cleanly when no section so the placeholder never leaks) + resolveRoutingSection (composition-scoped <dir>/.garrison/routing.json, falling back to the model-router seed config; compiled via a RUNTIME dynamic import of the fitting's pure routing-core.mjs by file URL — never pulled into the Next webpack bundle). assembleSystemPrompt calls it after {{capabilities}}; buildOrchestratorInstructions (the ~/.claude rules projection path) substitutes it too, so neither path leaks the placeholder. model-router ships .apm/prompts/model-router.prompt.md — a v4 routing-aware orchestrator carrying {{routing}} + {{capabilities}} + the dual reply contract ([route: …] + [orchestrator-active]). The live `default` composition still selects garrison-orchestrator (no {{routing}} placeholder → substitution is a no-op), so it is UNTOUCHED — proven by integration-check default still PASS. A new committed `compositions/router-v4/` fixture selects model-router and carries the generated assembled prompt (reused by MR1c/MR2). RETRY 1: a literal {{routing}} mention inside the prompt's explanatory HTML comment was being re-expanded by the global replace (duplicating the whole section + breaking the comment); reworded the comment to drop literal placeholders and added a 'marker appears exactly once' regression assertion. Satisfies brief §3 token assembly-ok.
